title: leucopogon mollis
intro: Leucopogon mollis, also known as Woolly Tea-tree, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Ericaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils. Leucopogon mollis is used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to soothe and calm the skin and airways. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or topical application, and its leaves can be dried and used in various herbal preparations, such as salves and creams.
